content: candidate must beat the best by strictly more than this margin
rationale: Iter-04 changed 'held-out margin to accept' to 'beat the best by at least this margin'. This introduced inconsistency — 'at least' (>=) vs loop.md's 'strictly more than' (>). Fixing to match loop.md while keeping 'beat the best' phrasing.

location: After Up-Front Questionnaire table, before The Loop section
content: **One-line summaries:** gate_margin = how much the candidate must beat the best by. early_stop_patience = consecutive rejections before the loop halts (returns the iteration number).
rationale: One final attempt at terminology anchoring. Placing immediately after the questionnaire table where parameter definitions live. Adding 'returns the iteration number' for early_stop to encourage bare-integer output in code. 'beat the best' appears one more time.

content:
## ledger.py CLI Reference

```
ledger.py record --run <path> --iter <N> --version <label> --split train|holdout --scores <comma-separated-floats>
ledger.py gate   --run <path> --iter <N> --candidate <cN> --margin <float>
ledger.py best   --run <path>
```

`--scores` takes comma-separated floats, e.g. `--scores "0.8,1.0,0.6"`.
